Optimize angle_based_smoother (#139): cache elem verts per iteration

human_actions_pending.action: Decide downstream propagation: ADMESH/QuADMesh/MADMESHing should consume v1.2.0 Phase 5 APIs

decisions.decision: Vectorized _build_adjacencies uses element-major slot-minor traversal (conn.ravel + np.roll)
decisions.rationale: first-encounter edge ID ordering must match the C++ backend; slot-major order broke test_cpp_layer_member_sets_match_python[bEdgeIDs]

decisions.decision: Edge IDs preserved via np.unique(return_index) + argsort(first_occur), not raw lexicographic order
decisions.rationale: C++/Python equivalence tests compare bEdgeIDs and Vert2Edge by edge ID

decisions.decision: angle_based_smoother optimized by caching, not full vectorization
decisions.rationale: per-vertex variable ring/incident-element counts resist clean array vectorization; caching elem verts per iteration is the high-leverage, low-risk win

next_action: Pick next open issue. #163 (Rust/C++ backends) aligns with user's stated direction on patchwork backends. Or propagate v1.2.0 to downstream repos.
context_notes: Phase 5 fully shipped + merged. All 939 tests pass. The optimization sweep (#164, #139) directly serves the user's #94 goal: faster CHILmesh primitives propagate to ADMESH/QuADMesh/MADMESHing.
